1вҶ’# Plan Review Rules
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’- Before presenting a plan to the user, review it using the codex command.
     4вҶ’- Adjust the review prompt as needed. However, codex tends to nitpick trivial things, so always include: "No nitpicks. Only flag critical issues."
     5вҶ’- Iterate update вҶ’ review until codex raises no more issues.
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’```bash
     8вҶ’# initial plan review request
     9вҶ’# always specify model with -m (gpt-5.3-codex is optimal)
    10вҶ’codex exec -m gpt-5.3-codex "Review this plan. No nitpicks. Only flag critical issues: {plan_full_path} (ref: {CLAUDE.md full_path})"
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’# updated plan review request
    13вҶ’# without resume --last, the context from the first review is lost
    14вҶ’codex exec resume --last -m gpt-5.3-codex "Plan updated. Review again. No nitpicks. Only flag critical issues: {plan_full_path} (ref: {CLAUDE.md full_path})"
    15вҶ’```
    16вҶ’

     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’# Commit Message Convention
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’Follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) with **required scope**.
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’Format: `<type>(<scope>): <description>`
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’## Types
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’- `feat` - New feature
    15вҶ’- `fix` - Bug fix
    16вҶ’- `docs` - Documentation changes
    17вҶ’- `build` - Build system, dependencies, Nix flake
    18вҶ’- `chore` - Maintenance tasks
    19вҶ’- `ci` - CI/CD configuration
    20вҶ’- `refactor` - Code restructuring without behavior change
    21вҶ’- `test` - Adding or updating tests
    22вҶ’- `perf` - Performance improvements
    23вҶ’- `style` - Code style (formatting, semicolons, etc.)
    24вҶ’- `revert` - Reverting a previous commit
    25вҶ’
    26вҶ’## Scope
    27вҶ’
    28вҶ’Scope is **mandatory**. Use the most relevant area for the project. Each project's CLAUDE.md lists its available scopes.
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’## PR Titles
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’PR titles **must** follow the same convention. Squash merges use the PR title as the commit message.
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’## Bad Examples
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’```
    37вҶ’feat: add markdown output        # missing scope
    38вҶ’update code                      # missing type and scope
    39вҶ’fix(github): Fix bug.            # don't capitalize, don't end with period
    40вҶ’Core: CLI + GitHub гғ—гғӯгғҗгӮӨгғҖгғј  # PR title not following convention
    41вҶ’```
    42вҶ’

1вҶ’# dotnix
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’[![DeepWiki](https://img.shields.io/badge/DeepWiki-yutakobayashidev%2Fdotnix-blue.svg?logo=data:image/png;base64,REDACTED/REDACTED/uv1saEDv4O3n3dV60RfP947Mm9/REDACTED//PB8mnKqScAhsD0kYP3j/REDACTED/56ebRWeraTjMt/REDACTED/REDACTED/REDACTED/REDACTED/REDACTED/REDACTED/REDACTED/cKaoqr+27/XcrS5UwSMbQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==)](https://deepwiki.com/yutakobayashidev/dotnix)
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Target
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’| Machine | Name | OS | System | Stable |
     8вҶ’|---|---|---|---|---|
     9вҶ’| UM790 Pro | UM790-Pro | NixOS | x86_64-linux | в—Һ |
    10вҶ’| Still Legend x870 | Still-Legend-x870 | NixOS (WSL) | x86_64-linux | в–і |
    11вҶ’| M2 MacBook Air | M2-MacBook-Air | macOS | aarch64-darwin | в—Һ |
    12вҶ’| Galaxy S23 FE | Galaxy-S23FE | Android (nix-on-droid) | aarch64-linux | в–і |
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’## Module Structure
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’```
    18вҶ’flake.nix                    # Entry point (nixos + darwin + nixOnDroid Configurations)
    19вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ nix/
    20вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ hosts/
    21вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ UM790-Pro/           # NixOS host config (boot, network, locale)
    22вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Still-Legend-x870/   # NixOS-WSL host config (WSL, locale)
    23вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ M2-MacBook-Air/      # macOS host config
    24вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ Galaxy-S23FE/        # nix-on-droid host config
    25вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ profiles/
    26вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cli-minimal.nix  # Minimal CLI environment
    27вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cli.nix          # CLI environment (docker, tailscale)
    28вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cli-server.nix   # Server CLI environment (docker, no tailscale)
    29вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ gui.nix          # GUI environment (niri, audio, bluetooth)
    30вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ laptop.nix       # Laptop environment (gui + extras)
    31вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ darwin.nix       # macOS environment
    32вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ modules/
    33вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ linux/           # NixOS system modules (niri, docker, audio, etc.)
    34вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ darwin/          # macOS nix-darwin modules (homebrew, system defaults, nix)
    35вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ nix-on-droid/    # nix-on-droid shared modules
    36вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ home/            # Home Manager shared modules (zsh, git, claude-code)
    37вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ overlays/            # Custom packages (overlay)
    38вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ agents/skills/           # Claude Code agent skills
    39вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ nvim/                    # Neovim config (Lua)
    40вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ zsh/                     # Zsh config
    41вҶ’```
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’## Documentation
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’- [docs/UM790Pro.md](docs/UM790Pro.md) - NixOS installation guide
    46вҶ’- [docs/Still-Legend-x870.md](docs/Still-Legend-x870.md) - NixOS-WSL installation guide
    47вҶ’- [docs/M2-MacBook-Air.md](docs/M2-MacBook-Air.md) - nix-darwin installation guide for macOS
    48вҶ’- [docs/Galaxy-S23FE.md](docs/Galaxy-S23FE.md) - nix-on-droid installation guide for Android
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’## Daily Usage
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’```sh
    53вҶ’# Apply changes (NixOS or macOS)
    54вҶ’nix run .#switch
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’# Build without applying
    57вҶ’nix run .#build
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’# Format all files (nix, lua, sh)
    60вҶ’nix run .#fmt
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’# Update flake inputs
    63вҶ’nix flake update
    64вҶ’```
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’## Available Nix Apps
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’### NixOS
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’- `nix run .#switch` - Build and apply NixOS + Home Manager configuration (`sudo nixos-rebuild switch`)
    71вҶ’- `nix run .#build` - Build configuration without applying
    72вҶ’- `nix run .#fmt` - Format all files (nix, lua, sh) via [treefmt](https://github.com/numtide/treefmt-nix)
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’### macOS
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’- `nix run .#switch` - Build and apply nix-darwin + Home Manager configuration (`darwin-rebuild switch`)
    77вҶ’- `nix run .#build` - Build configuration without applying
    78вҶ’- `nix run .#fmt` - Format all files (nix, lua, sh) via [treefmt](https://github.com/numtide/treefmt-nix)
    79вҶ’
    80вҶ’Both use [nix-output-monitor](https://github.com/maralorn/nix-output-monitor) for build output.
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’## Key Features
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’### NixOS
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’- **WM**: [Niri](https://github.com/YaLTeR/niri) (scrollable tiling Wayland compositor)
    87вҶ’- **IME**: fcitx5 + [hazkey](https://github.com/aster-void/nix-hazkey) (LLM-powered Japanese input)
    88вҶ’- **YubiKey**: PAM U2F authentication (polkit, swaylock)
    89вҶ’- **Development**: Docker, Tailscale, Android development environment
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’### macOS
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’- **Homebrew**: GUI app management via casks (Ghostty, Chrome, OrbStack, etc.)
    94вҶ’- **brew-nix**: Homebrew cask packages managed as Nix packages (version pinning & rollback)
    95вҶ’- **Touch ID**: sudo authentication support
    96вҶ’- **1Password**: Shell Plugins (gh, awscli2)
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’## Managed Tools
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’- **AI Development**: claude-code, codex, opencode, ccusage, vibe-kanban

# Agent Guidelines

Always prefer simplicity over pathological correctness. YAGNI, KISS, DRY. No backward-compat shims or fallback paths unless they come free without adding cyclomatic complexity.

## Pre-Commit Doc Check

Before committing, verify that the following docs reflect your changes:

- `README.md` вҖ” update if architecture, commands, or feature lists changed
- `AGENTS.md` вҖ” update if agent-facing instructions or context changed
- `CLAUDE.md` вҖ” update if Claude Code instructions changed
- `docs/` вҖ” update any related documentation

Skip if the change has no doc impact.

## Workflow Orchestration

### 1. Plan Mode Default

* Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions)
* If something goes sideways, stop and re-plan immediately вҖ” don't keep pushing
* Use plan mode for verification steps, not just building
* Write detailed specs upfront to reduce ambiguity

### 2. Subagent Strategy

* Use subagents liberally to keep main context window clean
* Offload research, exploration, and parallel analysis to subagents
* For complex problems, throw more compute at it via subagents
* One task per subagent for focused execution

### 3. Self-Improvement Loop

* After ANY correction from the user: update `tasks/lessons.md` with the pattern
* Write rules for yourself that prevent the same mistake
* Ruthlessly iterate on these lessons until mistake rate drops
* Review lessons at session start for relevant project

### 4. Verification Before Done

* Never mark a task complete without proving it works
* Diff behavior between main and your changes when relevant
* Ask yourself: "Would a staff engineer approve this?"
* Run tests, check logs, demonstrate correctness

### 5. Demand Elegance (Balanced)

* For non-trivial changes: pause and ask "is there a more elegant way?"
* If a fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
* Skip this for simple, obvious fixes вҖ” don't over-engineer
* Challenge your own work before presenting it

### 6. Autonomous Bug Fixing

* When given a bug report: just fix it. Don't ask for hand-holding
* Point at logs, errors, failing tests вҖ” then resolve them
* Zero context switching required from the user
* Go fix failing CI tests without being told how

## Task Management

1. **Plan First**: Write plan to `tasks/todo.md` with checkable items
2. **Verify Plan**: Check in before starting implementation
3. **Track Progress**: Mark items complete as you go
4. **Explain Changes**: High-level summary at each step
5. **Document Results**: Add review section to `tasks/todo.md`
6. **Capture Lessons**: Update `tasks/lessons.md` after corrections

## Core Principles

* **Simplicity First**: Make every change as simple as possible. Impact minimal code.
* **No Laziness**: Find root causes. No temporary fixes. Senior developer standards.
* **Minimal Impact**: Changes should only touch what's necessary. Avoid introducing bugs.

# Commit Message Convention

Follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) with **required scope**.

Format: `<type>(<scope>): <description>`

## Types

- `feat` - New feature
- `fix` - Bug fix
- `docs` - Documentation changes
- `build` - Build system, dependencies, Nix flake
- `chore` - Maintenance tasks
- `ci` - CI/CD configuration
- `refactor` - Code restructuring without behavior change
- `test` - Adding or updating tests
- `perf` - Performance improvements
- `style` - Code style (formatting, semicolons, etc.)
- `revert` - Reverting a previous commit

## Scope

Scope is **mandatory**. Use the most relevant area for the project. Each project's CLAUDE.md lists its available scopes.

## PR Titles

PR titles **must** follow the same convention. Squash merges use the PR title as the commit message.

## Bad Examples

```
feat: add markdown output        # missing scope
update code                      # missing type and scope
fix(github): Fix bug.            # don't capitalize, don't end with period
Core: CLI + GitHub гғ—гғӯгғҗгӮӨгғҖгғј  # PR title not following convention
```

# Agent Guidelines

Always prefer simplicity over pathological correctness. YAGNI, KISS, DRY. No backward-compat shims or fallback paths unless they come free without adding cyclomatic complexity.

## Pre-Commit Doc Check

Before committing, verify that the following docs reflect your changes:

- `README.md` вҖ” update if architecture, commands, or feature lists changed
- `AGENTS.md` вҖ” update if agent-facing instructions or context changed
- `CLAUDE.md` вҖ” update if Claude Code instructions changed
- `docs/` вҖ” update any related documentation

Skip if the change has no doc impact.

## Workflow Orchestration

### 1. Plan Mode Default

* Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions)
* If something goes sideways, stop and re-plan immediately вҖ” don't keep pushing
* Use plan mode for verification steps, not just building
* Write detailed specs upfront to reduce ambiguity

### 2. Subagent Strategy

* Use subagents liberally to keep main context window clean
* Offload research, exploration, and parallel analysis to subagents
* For complex problems, throw more compute at it via subagents
* One task per subagent for focused execution

### 3. Self-Improvement Loop

* After ANY correction from the user: update `tasks/lessons.md` with the pattern
* Write rules for yourself that prevent the same mistake
* Ruthlessly iterate on these lessons until mistake rate drops
* Review lessons at session start for relevant project

### 4. Verification Before Done

* Never mark a task complete without proving it works
* Diff behavior between main and your changes when relevant
* Ask yourself: "Would a staff engineer approve this?"
* Run tests, check logs, demonstrate correctness

### 5. Demand Elegance (Balanced)

* For non-trivial changes: pause and ask "is there a more elegant way?"
* If a fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
* Skip this for simple, obvious fixes вҖ” don't over-engineer
* Challenge your own work before presenting it

### 6. Autonomous Bug Fixing

* When given a bug report: just fix it. Don't ask for hand-holding
* Point at logs, errors, failing tests вҖ” then resolve them
* Zero context switching required from the user
* Go fix failing CI tests without being told how

## Task Management

1. **Plan First**: Write plan to `tasks/todo.md` with checkable items
2. **Verify Plan**: Check in before starting implementation
3. **Track Progress**: Mark items complete as you go
4. **Explain Changes**: High-level summary at each step
5. **Document Results**: Add review section to `tasks/todo.md`
6. **Capture Lessons**: Update `tasks/lessons.md` after corrections

## Core Principles

* **Simplicity First**: Make every change as simple as possible. Impact minimal code.
* **No Laziness**: Find root causes. No temporary fixes. Senior developer standards.
* **Minimal Impact**: Changes should only touch what's necessary. Avoid introducing bugs.

# Agent Guidelines

Always prefer simplicity over pathological correctness. YAGNI, KISS, DRY. No backward-compat shims or fallback paths unless they come free without adding cyclomatic complexity.

## Pre-Commit Doc Check

Before committing, verify that the following docs reflect your changes:

- `README.md` вҖ” update if architecture, commands, or feature lists changed
- `z-ai/AGENTS.md` вҖ” update if agent-facing instructions or context changed
- `z-ai/CLAUDE.md` вҖ” update if Claude Code instructions changed
- `docs/` вҖ” update any related documentation

Skip if the change has no doc impact.

## Workflow Orchestration

### 1. Plan Mode Default

* Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions)
* If something goes sideways, stop and re-plan immediately вҖ” don't keep pushing
* Use plan mode for verification steps, not just building
* Write detailed specs upfront to reduce ambiguity

### 2. Subagent Strategy

* Use subagents liberally to keep main context window clean
* Offload research, exploration, and parallel analysis to subagents
* For complex problems, throw more compute at it via subagents
* One task per subagent for focused execution

### 3. Self-Improvement Loop

* After ANY correction from the user: update `z-ai/tasks/lessons.md` with the pattern
* Write rules for yourself that prevent the same mistake
* Ruthlessly iterate on these lessons until mistake rate drops
* Review lessons at session start for relevant project

### 4. Verification Before Done

* Never mark a task complete without proving it works
* Diff behavior between main and your changes when relevant
* Ask yourself: "Would a staff engineer approve this?"
* Run tests, check logs, demonstrate correctness

### 5. Demand Elegance (Balanced)

* For non-trivial changes: pause and ask "is there a more elegant way?"
* If a fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
* Skip this for simple, obvious fixes вҖ” don't over-engineer
* Challenge your own work before presenting it

### 6. Autonomous Bug Fixing

* When given a bug report: just fix it. Don't ask for hand-holding
* Point at logs, errors, failing tests вҖ” then resolve them
* Zero context switching required from the user
* Go fix failing CI tests without being told how

## Task Management

1. **Plan First**: Write plan to `z-ai/tasks/todo.md` with checkable items
2. **Verify Plan**: Check in before starting implementation
3. **Track Progress**: Mark items complete as you go
4. **Explain Changes**: High-level summary at each step
5. **Document Results**: Add review section to `tasks/todo.md`
6. **Capture Lessons**: Update `tasks/lessons.md` after corrections

## Core Principles

* **Simplicity First**: Make every change as simple as possible. Impact minimal code.
* **No Laziness**: Find root causes. No temporary fixes. Senior developer standards.
* **Minimal Impact**: Changes should only touch what's necessary. Avoid introducing bugs.

# Agent Guidelines

Always prefer simplicity over pathological correctness. YAGNI, KISS, DRY. No backward-compat shims or fallback paths unless they come free without adding cyclomatic complexity.

## Pre-Commit Doc Check

Before committing, verify that the following docs reflect your changes:

- `README.md` вҖ” update if architecture, commands, or feature lists changed
- `AGENTS.md` вҖ” update if agent-facing instructions or context changed
- `CLAUDE.md` вҖ” update if Claude Code instructions changed
- `docs/` вҖ” update any related documentation

Skip if the change has no doc impact.

## Workflow Orchestration

### 1. Plan Mode Default

* Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions)
* If something goes sideways, stop and re-plan immediately вҖ” don't keep pushing
* Use plan mode for verification steps, not just building
* Write detailed specs upfront to reduce ambiguity

### 2. Subagent Strategy

* Use subagents liberally to keep main context window clean
* Offload research, exploration, and parallel analysis to subagents
* For complex problems, throw more compute at it via subagents
* One task per subagent for focused execution

### 3. Self-Improvement Loop

* After ANY correction from the user: update `tasks/lessons.md` with the pattern
* Write rules for yourself that prevent the same mistake
* Ruthlessly iterate on these lessons until mistake rate drops
* Review lessons at session start for relevant project

### 4. Verification Before Done

* Never mark a task complete without proving it works
* Diff behavior between main and your changes when relevant
* Ask yourself: "Would a staff engineer approve this?"
* Run tests, check logs, demonstrate correctness

### 5. Demand Elegance (Balanced)

* For non-trivial changes: pause and ask "is there a more elegant way?"
* If a fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
* Skip this for simple, obvious fixes вҖ” don't over-engineer
* Challenge your own work before presenting it

### 6. Autonomous Bug Fixing

* When given a bug report: just fix it. Don't ask for hand-holding
* Point at logs, errors, failing tests вҖ” then resolve them
* Zero context switching required from the user
* Go fix failing CI tests without being told how

## Task Management

1. **Plan First**: Write plan to `tasks/todo.md` with checkable items
2. **Verify Plan**: Check in before starting implementation
3. **Track Progress**: Mark items complete as you go
4. **Explain Changes**: High-level summary at each step
5. **Document Results**: Add review section to `tasks/todo.md`
6. **Capture Lessons**: Update `tasks/lessons.md` after corrections

## Core Principles

* **Simplicity First**: Make every change as simple as possible. Impact minimal code.
* **No Laziness**: Find root causes. No temporary fixes. Senior developer standards.
* **Minimal Impact**: Changes should only touch what's necessary. Avoid introducing bugs.

# Agent Guidelines

Always prefer simplicity over pathological correctness. YAGNI, KISS, DRY. No backward-compat shims or fallback paths unless they come free without adding cyclomatic complexity.

## Pre-Commit Doc Check

Before committing, verify that the following docs reflect your changes:

- `README.md` вҖ” update if architecture, commands, or feature lists changed
- `AGENTS.md` вҖ” update if agent-facing instructions or context changed
- `CLAUDE.md` вҖ” update if Claude Code instructions changed
- `docs/` вҖ” update any related documentation

Skip if the change has no doc impact.

## Workflow Orchestration

### 1. Plan Mode Default

* Enter plan mode for ANY non-trivial task (3+ steps or architectural decisions)
* If something goes sideways, stop and re-plan immediately вҖ” don't keep pushing
* Use plan mode for verification steps, not just building
* Write detailed specs upfront to reduce ambiguity

### 2. Subagent Strategy

* Use subagents liberally to keep main context window clean
* Offload research, exploration, and parallel analysis to subagents
* For complex problems, throw more compute at it via subagents
* One task per subagent for focused execution

### 3. Self-Improvement Loop

* After ANY correction from the user: update `z-ai/lessons.md` with the pattern
* Write rules for yourself that prevent the same mistake
* Ruthlessly iterate on these lessons until mistake rate drops
* Review lessons at session start for relevant project

### 4. Verification Before Done

* Never mark a task complete without proving it works
* Diff behavior between main and your changes when relevant
* Ask yourself: "Would a staff engineer approve this?"
* Run tests, check logs, demonstrate correctness

### 5. Demand Elegance (Balanced)

* For non-trivial changes: pause and ask "is there a more elegant way?"
* If a fix feels hacky: "Knowing everything I know now, implement the elegant solution"
* Skip this for simple, obvious fixes вҖ” don't over-engineer
* Challenge your own work before presenting it

### 6. Autonomous Bug Fixing

* When given a bug report: just fix it. Don't ask for hand-holding
* Point at logs, errors, failing tests вҖ” then resolve them
* Zero context switching required from the user
* Go fix failing CI tests without being told how

## Task Management

1. **Plan First**: Write plan to `z-ai/todo.md` with checkable items
2. **Verify Plan**: Check in before starting implementation
3. **Track Progress**: Mark items complete as you go
4. **Explain Changes**: High-level summary at each step
5. **Document Results**: Add review section to `z-ai/todo.md`
6. **Capture Lessons**: Update `z-ai/lessons.md` after corrections

## Core Principles

* **Simplicity First**: Make every change as simple as possible. Impact minimal code.
* **No Laziness**: Find root causes. No temporary fixes. Senior developer standards.
* **Minimal Impact**: Changes should only touch what's necessary. Avoid introducing bugs.
